claim_text: Although LPIPS and other DFDs[28–33] outperform commonly used IQ metrics in terms of correlation with human perceptual judgments in large-scale computer vision studies,[34] these studies evaluate DFDs on natural images belonging to the same domain as the images used for encoder training.

sub_claims.text: cheon2021 is a representative perceptual IQ / DFD work — it proposes a deep-feature-based full-reference perceptual image quality assessment method (IQT, an Image Quality Transformer that builds on CNN-extracted perceptual feature representations) and benchmarks it against LPIPS and other deep-feature distance / perceptual metrics on standard IQA datasets, fitting the manuscript's framing of an LPIPS-and-other-DFDs perceptual-IQ study on natural images.

evidence.snippet: we propose an image quality transformer (IQT) that successfully applies a transformer architecture to a perceptual full-reference image quality assessment (IQA) task. Perceptual representation becomes more important in image quality assessment. In this context, we extract the perceptual feature representations from each of input images using a convolutional neural network (CNN) backbone.

Perceptual image quality assessment (IQA) is an important topic in the multimedia systems and computer vision tasks [11, 42, 56]. One of the goals of the image processing is to improve the quality of the content to an acceptable level for the human viewers.

The existing objective metrics such as Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR), a structural similarity index (SSIM) [51], and conventional quality metrics are insufficient to predict the quality of this kind of outputs. In this respect, recent works [61, 16, 21, 39, 3] based on perceptual representation exhibit a better performance at the perceptual IQA task.

In this study, we propose an Image Quality Transformer (IQT), which is the FR image quality assessment method ... To tackle the perceptual aspects, a convolutional neural network (CNN) backbone is used to extract perceptual representations from an input image.

Recently, CNN-based IQA methods as well as other low-level computer vision tasks have been actively studied [61, 3, 39, 17, 24]. Zhang et al. proposed a learned perceptual image patch similarity (LPIPS) metric [61] for FR-IQA. The LPIPS showed that trained deep features that are optimized by the Euclidean distance between distorted and reference images are effective for IQA compared to the conventional IQA methods. Ding et al. proposed the metric that is robust to texture resampling and geometric transformation based on spatial averages of the feature maps [16]. Various IQA methods including aforementioned metrics are included in our experiments for performance comparison.
Feature Extraction Backbone. A conventional CNN network, Inception-Resnet-V2 [44], is employed as the feature extraction backbone network. Pretrained weights on ImageNet [14] is imported and frozen.

We employ five databases that are commonly used in the research of perceptual image quality assessment. The LIVE Image Quality Assessment Database (LIVE) [42], the Categorical Subjective Image Quality (CSIQ) database [32], and the TID2013 [38] are the databases that serve as baselines for full-reference IQA studies. ... KADID-10k [33] is a large-scale IQA dataset and is chosen as the training dataset in our experiment. ... The PIPAL [22] dataset is used for both the training and evaluation of the model in this study. A large quantity of distorted images including GAN based algorithms' outputs and following human ratings are included in the PIPAL dataset.

This work is proposed to participate in the NTIRE 2021 perceptual image quality assessment challenge [23]. The objective of this challenge is to develop a model predicting a value with high accuracy comparable to the ground-truth MOS. ... Our model won the first place in terms of the main score among all participants.

We proposed an IQT and it is appropriately applied to a perceptual image quality assessment task by taking an advantage of transformer encoder-decoder architecture. The IQT demonstrated the outstanding performance on the three standard IQA databases compared to many existing methods. Our method also showed the best performance for the latest IQA dataset that contains deep learning-based distorted images.

sub_claims.nuance: Cheon et al. 2021 ('Perceptual Image Quality Assessment with Transformers', CVPR Workshops 2021 — NTIRE) proposes the Image Quality Transformer (IQT), a perceptual full-reference IQA method that extracts perceptual feature representations from a CNN backbone (Inception-ResNet-V2 pretrained on ImageNet, with frozen weights) and feeds them through a transformer encoder-decoder. It (a) is itself a deep-feature-based perceptual quality predictor and (b) directly benchmarks against LPIPS, DISTS, PieAPP, WaDIQaM, and other deep-feature / perceptual metrics on standard IQA datasets (LIVE, CSIQ, TID2013, KADID-10k, PIPAL) using SRCC/KRCC/PLCC against human MOS ratings on natural images. This squarely fits the manuscript's grouping of 'LPIPS and other DFDs' as representative perceptual-IQ / DFD work evaluated on natural images. Note: cheon2021 is one of references [28-33] in the manuscript's enumerated DFD list; this evidence file establishes only that the source paper qualifies as a representative perceptual IQ / DFD work, not the broader composite claim about 'large-scale computer vision studies' or 'same-domain' evaluation framing, which is sourced from [34] and the joint enumeration. IQT is more accurately a 'perceptual IQA model that uses deep features end-to-end through a transformer head' than a pure feature-distance metric in the strict LPIPS / DISTS sense, but is consistently grouped with LPIPS-style perceptual quality predictors in the IQA literature.

phrasings_tried: perceptual IQA / perceptual image quality assessment (paper title and abstract framing; perceptual representation extraction)
deep feature distance / DFD (CNN-extracted perceptual feature representations; learned perceptual image patch similarity; deep features optimized by Euclidean distance)
LPIPS (LPIPS-Alex/LPIPS-VGG benchmarked alongside the proposed IQT in standard IQA tables)
full-reference IQA (FR-IQA) on natural-image IQA benchmarks (LIVE/CSIQ/TID2013/KADID-10k/PIPAL)
correlation with human MOS (SRCC/KRCC/PLCC against subjective mean opinion scores)
attestation.indirect_attribution_check: cheon2021 is a primary methods paper that directly proposes the IQT perceptual IQA model and benchmarks itself against LPIPS and other perceptual / deep-feature-based metrics. It does not relay an attribution from another primary; the paper is itself the source for being a representative perceptual IQ / DFD work. Direct attribution is appropriate.
attestation.out_of_context_check: The manuscript groups cheon2021 in [28-33] as 'LPIPS and other DFDs' — i.e., as a representative deep-feature-distance / perceptual-IQ work on natural images. cheon2021 itself is a CNN-feature-based transformer perceptual IQA model evaluated on standard natural-image IQA databases (LIVE, CSIQ, TID2013, KADID-10k, PIPAL) with human MOS ratings as ground truth. While IQT is more accurately a 'perceptual IQA model that uses deep features end-to-end through a transformer head' rather than a pure feature-distance metric in the strict LPIPS / DISTS sense, it is consistently grouped with LPIPS-style perceptual quality predictors in the IQA literature and explicitly benchmarks against them, so the manuscript's grouping of cheon2021 with 'LPIPS and other DFDs' on natural images is in-context for this representative-citation framing. No out-of-context concern.
